chunks.text: Ana Arias, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) and Chair of the Electrical Engineering (EE) Division, has been elevated to the rank of IEEE Fellow. This honor is bestowed upon individuals who demonstrate 'unusual distinction in the profession,' recognizing their exceptional contributions to the field of engineering.
chunks.question: What honor did Ana Arias receive?

chunks.text: UC Berkeley announces AI Center of Excellence. The center will serve as a hub for cutting-edge applied research in generative AI (GenAI), drawing on the collective knowledge and resources of Intel and UC Berkeley.
chunks.question: What is the purpose of UC Berkeley's AI Center of Excellence?

chunks.text: EECS grad students Anastasios Angelopoulos and Wei-Lin Chiang were profiled by The Wall Street Journal for their work on Chatbot Arena.
chunks.question: Why were Anastasios Angelopoulos and Wei-Lin Chiang profiled?

chunks.text: EECS graduate students Rahul Iyer and Tahmid Mahbub have received the IEEE INTELEC-2024 Best Paper Award for their innovative work on energy systems in communications.
chunks.question: What award did Rahul Iyer and Tahmid Mahbub win?

chunks.text: Robots could soon play a larger role in surgery thanks to recent AI developments, as discussed in a new Science Robotics paper. This aims to help close surgeons' skill gaps and improve patient outcomes.
chunks.question: How can robots improve surgery?

chunks.text: EECS Associate Professor Rikky Muller is part of a team creating implantable bioelectronic devices to address obesity and diabetes through the ROGUE project, funded by ARPA-H awards.
chunks.question: What is the ROGUE project's goal?

chunks.text: Public trust in U.S. elections is decreasing, but UC Berkeley scholars note that elections are more secure and results are more reliable than perceived.
chunks.question: What do UC Berkeley scholars say about U.S. elections?

chunks.text: CS Associate Professor Alvin Cheung has been awarded the Dahl-Nygaard Junior Prize for his groundbreaking work on 'verified lifting,' improving database-backed applications.
chunks.question: Why did Alvin Cheung win the Dahl-Nygaard Junior Prize?

chunks.text: The oral history of William 'Bill' Oldham, professor emeritus of EECS, is now online in the UC Berkeley Library's digital collections. He was an expert on process technology.
chunks.question: What is available online about Bill Oldham?

chunks.text: Hany Farid, a UC Berkeley professor, is a digital forensics expert who reviews media to detect deepfakes, helping combat political misinformation.
chunks.question: What does Hany Farid do as a digital forensics expert?
